perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as compelling a Party to disclose any Confidential Information to the other, or to enter into any further contractual relationship with the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as compelling a Party to disclose any Information to the other, or to enter into any further contractual relationship with the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information', the modified text broadens the scope to 'any Information'. This contradicts the initial definition in Section 3, which limits the scope of the agreement to 'Confidential Information'. This creates uncertainty about what information is protected under the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in the Agreement the term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information or data whether of financial, commercial, technical, legal or whatever nature disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party under this Agreement. However, that information disclosed by the Disclosing Party hereunder shall not be Confidential Information if it is, as proven by the Receiving Party by appropriate documentation: (a) already available to the public or becomes available to the public through no breach of the Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (b) in the Receiving Party's possession prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (c) received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to the Receiving Party; or (d) the result of developments undertaken by the Receiving Party’s personnel which had no access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is specifically defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text broadens this to 'any Information'. This contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which limits the scope to information disclosed 'under this Agreement' and provides exceptions. By removing 'Confidential', the changed text implies protection for all information, regardless of whether it falls under the defined 'Confidential Information' or the exceptions, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will not retain any copies, extracts or reproductions in whole or in part of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will not retain any sensitive documents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'sensitive documents' introduces ambiguity and shifts the focus. What constitutes a 'sensitive document' is subjective and undefined within the contract. The term 'Confidential Information' is specifically defined in section 3. Now, the receiving party's requirement to return documentation is now ambigious and contraditory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information, all copies thereof and all rights thereto, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. All Confidential Information, whether original or copies thereof, including any document, note, meeting report containing such information, shall be promptly returned by the Receiving Party to the Disclosing Party on receipt of the Disclosing Party's written request therefor. The Receiving Party will not retain any sensitive documents.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'The Receiving Party will not retain any copies, extracts or reproductions in whole or in part of the Confidential Information.' which aligns with the requirement to return all 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text. The changed text states 'The Receiving Party will not retain any sensitive documents'. The 'Confidential Information' is well defined, whereas 'sensitive documents' is not. The term 'sensitive documents' is now used when previously 'Confidential Information' was used, causing a contradiction between what should be returned and what should not be retained.

perturbation.original_text: not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information' in this clause, the restriction is broadened to include any kind of information. This contradicts the initial definition of Confidential Information in section 3. This makes article 4, which lays out the duties of the receiving party, contraditory with respect to the agreement that is being signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in the Agreement the term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information or data whether of financial, commercial, technical, legal or whatever nature disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party under this Agreement. However, that information disclosed by the Disclosing Party hereunder shall not be Confidential Information if it is, as proven by the Receiving Party by appropriate documentation: (a) already available to the public or becomes available to the public through no breach of the Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (b) in the Receiving Party's possession prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (c) received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to the Receiving Party; or (d) the result of developments undertaken by the Receiving Party’s personnel which had no access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the claiming, registering, or exercising of rights on 'Confidential Information'. The changed text broadens this restriction to include 'information' without the 'Confidential' qualifier. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which defines 'Confidential Information' specifically, creating a contradiction. The altered clause now potentially covers any information, conflicting with the defined scope of 'Confidential Information' elsewhere in the agreement, therefore, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to the Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'Confidential' again broadens the scope to 'Information', removing the link to the defined term and widening liability. This creates an in-text contradiction between the original intent of the contract and the responsibilities of the parties with respect to the agreement being signed
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in the Agreement the term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information or data whether of financial, commercial, technical, legal or whatever nature disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party under this Agreement. However, that information disclosed by the Disclosing Party hereunder shall not be Confidential Information if it is, as proven by the Receiving Party by appropriate documentation: (a) already available to the public or becomes available to the public through no breach of the Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (b) in the Receiving Party's possession prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (c) received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to the Receiving Party; or (d) the result of developments undertaken by the Receiving Party’s personnel which had no access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which aligns with the definition in the contradicted text. The changed text broadens this to simply 'Information'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text specifically defines 'Confidential Information' with exclusions (a-d), while the changed text implies that all 'Information' is subject to the same disclaimers of warranties. This creates a contradiction, as now 'Information' is not tied to the original definition and its exceptions. Thus it qualifies as a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the laws of Belgium.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the arbitration clause and replacing it with 'shall be finally settled under the laws of Belgium' creates a contradiction. The original clause specifies international arbitration in Paris, while the modified clause defaults to Belgian law, without detailing the specific method of resolution. Furthermore, the last sentence in that section makes a double standard where it indicates 'The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.' now this line makes little to no sense, since the arbitration has been removed from the clause, causing a clear in-text contradiction and confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the laws of Belgium. The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear method for dispute resolution (international arbitration in Paris), while the changed text replaces this with 'shall be finally settled under the laws of Belgium,' which introduces ambiguity regarding the specific dispute resolution method. The contradicted text includes the sentence 'The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.' However, because the changed text removed the arbitration clause, this sentence becomes nonsensical and creates an in-text contradiction, as there is no longer an arbitration process to refer to. This represents a strong contradiction due to the logical inconsistency and uncertainty about how disputes will be resolved.
